behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: this failure appears at the watcher↔OS filesystem-event boundary (recursive directory monitoring and real emitted events). A fake callback can miss real watcher behavior for nested paths. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only filesystem watching (no AI decision, no multi-agent chain), so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file into that subdirectory, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior text targets deduplication logic itself (same path + time window), which is local decision logic and does not require module handoff to reproduce. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: text does not require real watcher/OS events; it specifies logic over 'same file path within a configurable time window,' which can be exercised in-process with a controlled clock.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the dedup component with a test clock and configured window; submit request A for path P, then request B for path P within window and assert B is discarded; advance clock past window and assert request C for P is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate events; this is a real filesystem-event condition. MOCK-HIDE: mocked single callback patterns can hide duplicate-event burst behavior from actual OS/watch backend. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: no AI/model output and no multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real watched directory, perform a file write pattern that triggers multiple events on the host OS (e.g., temp-write + rename/append), then assert only one pipeline execution/output is produced for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: directory deletion/recreation watcher continuity is OS-level behavior (watch handle invalidation/rebind). MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes often preserve subscriptions and miss real rewatch failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: behavior is infrastructure resilience only, with no AI decision chain; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real watched directory, remove that directory while daemon is alive, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: 'arrive at the same time' plus 'creation timestamps' depends on real filesystem timestamp behavior/resolution and event timing under OS scheduling. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing timestamps can make ordering always clean and miss real tie/race behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: no AI output quality dependency and no multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real filesystem, create several files in a tight burst, capture observed processing order from daemon logs/output metadata, and assert order follows filesystem creation timestamps (with explicit tie-break expectation if timestamps are equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: partial-write timing is a real OS/filesystem condition involving writer/reader concurrency. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked 'file ready' state can hide chunked-write races and premature pickup. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels: behavior is filesystem/process coordination only; no AI decision-making or multi-agent pipeline, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, open a large file for incremental writes in the watched directory, trigger arrival event on first chunk, continue writing chunks, then close file; assert daemon begins processing only after final write/close condition and output reflects full file contents.
